api/client/python/examples.purpose: Runnable Python example scripts (sync/ and async/) demonstrating openmeter SDK usage against a live endpoint; each script is self-contained, exercises one domain (ingest, query, entitlement, subscription, customer), and doubles as documentation and a Pyright-checked regression surface for the generated SDK. The folder root holds only Poetry/Pyright config and a README.

patterns.name: Environment-variable configuration with defaults
patterns.description: Endpoint and token are always read from OPENMETER_ENDPOINT / OPENMETER_TOKEN via os.environ.get() with sensible defaults; never hardcoded.

patterns.name: HttpResponseError catch-all
patterns.description: Every SDK call is wrapped in try/except corehttp.exceptions.HttpResponseError to surface API errors consistently.

patterns.name: Dict-access for discriminated-union list items
patterns.description: Polymorphic list responses (e.g. entitlements) are plain dicts — use .get(), never attribute access.

patterns.name: One file per domain, self-contained
patterns.description: Each domain lives in its own file under sync/ or async/; no cross-domain imports or shared helpers between example files.

patterns.name: Client import split: async.aio vs sync top-level
patterns.description: Files under async/ import from openmeter.aio and use async with + asyncio.run(); sync/ files import from openmeter and call main() directly at module level.
patterns.example: from openmeter.aio import Client  # only in async/

patterns.name: Client lifetime: module-level (sync) vs context manager (async)
patterns.description: Sync scripts construct the blocking Client once at module level; async scripts open/close the HTTP session via async with client: inside the coroutine, never manually.
patterns.example: client = Client(endpoint=..., credential=...)  # top of sync/ingest.py ; async with client:  # inside async def main()

key_file_guides.role: Poetry project referencing the parent SDK via a path dependency (develop=true); pins python ^3.9 to match the SDK floor.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not add a version constraint on the openmeter path dependency — it must track the local checkout; do not raise the python floor beyond ^3.9 without checking SDK compatibility.

key_file_guides.role: Pyright config covering both async/ and sync/ at basic mode.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Mypy is unsupported (overloaded SDK constructors) — never add mypy config.

key_file_guides.role: Setup + run instructions (poetry install, env-var invocation, poetry run pyright).
key_file_guides.watch_for: States Pyright-only type checking — keep examples Pyright-clean.

key_file_guides.role: Child: async example scripts using openmeter.aio.Client with async with + asyncio.run().
key_file_guides.watch_for: asyncio.run() must wrap the top-level coroutine; calling inside a running loop raises RuntimeError.

key_file_guides.role: Child: structural mirror of async/ using the blocking openmeter.Client at module level.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must not use async with or import from openmeter.aio.

anti_patterns: Importing from openmeter.aio in sync/ examples or from openmeter (sync) in async/ examples
Hardcoding endpoint or token instead of reading OPENMETER_ENDPOINT / OPENMETER_TOKEN
Using attribute access on dict-typed discriminated-union responses instead of .get()
Adding cross-domain logic or shared helpers across example files — each must stay self-contained
Running type checks with mypy — incompatible with the SDK's overloaded constructors; use pyright only

decisions.decision: async/ and sync/ are structurally identical folders with separate client imports
decisions.rationale: Keeps both execution models discoverable and ensures every domain is covered in both without a shared abstraction obscuring per-client differences.
The generated SDK uses overloaded constructors mypy cannot handle; Pyright is the only supported static type checker.

decisions.decision: One file per domain, not a monolithic demo
decisions.rationale: Each domain script runs and reads independently, and new domain examples are added without touching existing files.

code_examples.scenario: Async ingest entry point — canonical async/ pattern
code_examples.code:
import asyncio, os
from corehttp.exceptions import HttpResponseError
from openmeter.aio import Client

client = Client(
    endpoint=os.environ.get('OPENMETER_ENDPOINT', 'https://openmeter.cloud'),
    credential=os.environ.get('OPENMETER_TOKEN', ''),
)

async def main():
    async with client:
        try:
            await client.ingest_events(...)
        except HttpResponseError as e:
            print(e)

asyncio.run(main())
